steps.title: Identify what the narrative opening must do
steps.content: The task asks for a narrative that begins with a specific athlete's story, using career details as a launching point. The opening must focus on one athlete, anchor itself in the start of that career, and use a concrete team reference from the notes (e.g., the New England Patriots).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Martin began his career with the New England Patriots; Brown started with Cleveland Browns.': Immediately splits focus between both athletes rather than opening with one. ✗
  • Choice B — 'When Martin put on his New England Patriots uniform for his first NFL game, he began what would become a legendary professional career.': Opens on Martin alone, anchors the narrative at the very start of his career with a concrete team detail, and signals future achievement. ✓
  • Choice C — 'While Martin finished his career with more rushing yards, both Martin and Brown reached the career milestone of rushing for over 10,000 yards total.': Compares both athletes and focuses on career outcomes, not the career's outset. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Martin's blend of speed, power, and agility helped him accrue 14,101 rushing yards by the end of his career, etching his name into the NFL's record books.': Focuses on the end of Martin's career rather than the beginning. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
